seo.title: Funding Structuring - vendor MDF, AWS co-sell & EU digital grants
seo.description: After the training comes implementation, and implementation needs budget. We structure your AI adoption program to qualify for Microsoft MDF, AWS co-sell, or EU digital grants.
seo.keywords: Microsoft MDF, AWS co-sell, EU digital grants, AI funding, Digital Europe Programme, AI grant Romania, vendor credits

hero.title: You learned what to build. Now fund building it.
hero.subtitle: Training gives your team the knowledge and the prototypes. Production - agents, integrations, Copilot and Azure licences, dev work, change management - costs real money. Vendor MDF, AWS co-sell and EU digital grants exist specifically to fund this. Most companies qualify. Few know how to apply.
hero.note: Free 30-min scoping call · Written shortlist in 5 business days · No commitment

steps.label: Training
steps.title: Your team learns to prototype with AI on real workflows.
steps.body: In our programmes, teams leave with working prototypes built on their own problems - not slideware. The knowledge stays in-house.

steps.label: The gap
steps.title: Prototypes are not production.
steps.body: Turning a prototype into a deployed agent or workflow needs licences, integrations, dev work, and change management. That's where most adoption stalls - not on knowledge, on budget.

steps.label: Funding
steps.title: Vendor MDF, AWS co-sell and EU grants exist to fund exactly this.
steps.body: We structure your adoption program so it qualifies - and we draft the application. The work happens either way; the funding decides whether it costs you anything.

intro.intro: This is the step that comes after the training. The knowledge is yours; the implementation needs budget. In most cases, the budget exists - it just hasn't been identified or structured correctly.
paragraphs: Every quarter, Microsoft, AWS, and Google allocate Market Development Funds to partners who drive platform adoption. These budgets exist specifically to support AI skills and implementation programs inside organisations.
Most of them go unused, not because companies don't qualify, but because nobody structured the program in a way that meets the funding criteria.

The same is true for EU digital competitiveness grants under Digital Europe and related programs. The applications fail not because the work isn't fundable, but because they're submitted as internal training plans instead of qualifying projects.
intro.italicStart: We turn your AI adoption program into something that qualifies.
intro.italicEm: You run the program. You get the cost back.

instruments.titleEm: Most companies have access to at least one.

items.title: Microsoft MDF & Founders Hub
items.audience: For Microsoft partners, Copilot rollouts, and eligible startups
items.body: Two tracks. Partners: Market Development Funds support Copilot, Azure AI, and Microsoft 365 adoption - programs must be structured around specific adoption outcomes, not generic training. Startups & scaleups: Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub provides up to $150k in Azure credits plus Copilot, GitHub, and OpenAI API access. We facilitate access to both tracks.
items.coverage: Copilot & Microsoft 365 licences, Azure AI consumption, OpenAI API credits via Founders Hub, partner-led adoption work, training & enablement

items.title: AWS co-sell & Activate credits
items.audience: For AWS partners, cloud-adjacent teams, and funded startups
items.body: Two tracks. APN partners: co-sell budgets fund digital skills and cloud adoption programs aligned to AWS adoption metrics. Startups & scaleups: AWS Activate provides up to $100k in credits (via VC, accelerator, or standalone application) plus Bedrock and SageMaker access. We facilitate access to both tracks.
items.coverage: Bedrock & SageMaker consumption, Activate credits up to $100k, migration credits, partner-led delivery, enablement workshops

items.title: Google Cloud partner & startup credits
items.audience: For Google Cloud partners, Workspace rollouts, and AI-first scaleups
items.body: Two tracks. Partners: Google Cloud Partner Advantage allocates co-marketing and adoption funds for Vertex AI, Gemini Enterprise, and Workspace rollouts. Startups & scaleups: Google for Startups Cloud Program offers up to $200k in Google Cloud credits plus Gemini API access and dedicated AI support. We facilitate access to both tracks.
items.coverage: Vertex AI & Gemini API consumption, Workspace + Gemini Enterprise licences, up to $200k cloud credits, partner-led delivery, MVP build credits

items.title: EU digital grants & Erasmus+
items.audience: Digital Europe, Horizon Europe, and Erasmus+ instruments
items.body: EU funding for AI literacy, digital skills, and implementation work inside organisations. Includes Digital Europe Programme (AI deployment projects), Horizon Europe digital pillars (R&D and innovation), and Erasmus+ (upskilling cohorts, training mobility, partnerships). Applications must be framed as qualifying projects with measurable outcomes, not internal training budgets.
items.coverage: AI implementation projects, MVP build & validation, upskilling cohorts under Erasmus+, Digital Europe AI deployment, integration & dev work, third-party advisory

steps.title: Audit your vendor relationships
steps.body: We review your current Microsoft Partner status, AWS tier, and any existing vendor agreements to identify which funding programs you qualify for right now.

steps.title: Identify eligible instruments
steps.body: Based on the audit, we map the specific MDF programs, co-sell budgets, or EU grant instruments that fit your situation. Not a generic list, a specific set of options with qualification criteria confirmed.

steps.title: Structure the program to qualify
steps.body: We redesign the scope and documentation of your AI adoption program to meet the funding criteria. This is the step most companies miss. The program content stays the same. The way it's framed and documented changes.

steps.title: Draft and support the application
steps.body: We write or co-author the MDF proposal or grant application. Vendor fund applications require specific language and metrics. We know what those are.

steps.title: Coordinate delivery and reimbursement
steps.body: Delivery documentation is aligned with program requirements from the start, not retrofitted at the end. Reimbursement doesn't get stuck because the paperwork doesn't match the application.

result.titleStart: A fully funded, or significantly reduced cost,
paragraphs: For companies where internal budget approval is the blocker, this removes the objection entirely.
For companies where budget exists but is hard to defend, qualifying for vendor or EU funding shifts the conversation from "should we spend this?" to "should we leave this on the table?"
result.italicEm: The funding determines whether it costs you anything.

quote.body: Most companies qualify for at least one instrument. The question is not whether the funding exists, it does. The question is whether your program is structured in a way that the funding body will recognise.

eventsBridge.titleStart: Want to see what a live session looks like
eventsBridge.body: Come into a live prototyping session and see exactly what the program produces - working prototypes, team capability, deliverables - before you structure the funding around it.

items.q: How long does an MDF application take?
items.a: From kick-off to submission, typically 3 to 5 weeks: 1 week of audit and instrument mapping, 1–2 weeks of program structuring, 1–2 weeks of drafting and partner sign-off. The vendor's own decision window is usually another 2–6 weeks on top.

items.q: What if we are not a Microsoft / AWS partner yet?
items.a: We still have options. EU digital grants don't require partner status. For Microsoft and AWS specifically, we either route through one of our partner relationships, or help you set up partner status in parallel - most companies that already pay for Copilot or AWS qualify for an entry tier within weeks.

items.q: How does the reimbursement actually work?
items.a: It depends on the instrument. Vendor MDF is usually paid against approved milestones - we structure delivery so the documentation matches the application from day one. EU grants are typically tranche-based against verified deliverables. In both cases the program runs first; the money lands after each verified milestone.

items.q: What documents do you need from us?
items.a: For the audit: your current vendor agreements (Microsoft Partner status, AWS tier), recent invoices for AI/cloud spend, and a short brief on the program you want to run. For the application itself: org details, headcount, target outcomes. We draft everything else.

The 30-minute scoping call and the written shortlist of instruments are free. If we move forward, structuring and application work is scoped per program - usually a fixed fee plus a success component tied to funding actually approved. We confirm the numbers before any commitment.

items.q: What if we don't qualify for anything?
items.a: Then we tell you in the shortlist, in writing, within 5 business days. No fee, no engagement. Most companies qualify for at least one instrument, but if the structure genuinely doesn't fit, we don't manufacture an application.

cta.title: Find out what you qualify for.
cta.subtitle: A 30-minute call to map your vendor relationships and EU eligibility. We come back with a written shortlist of instruments and estimated coverage within 5 business days. No commitment until you see the numbers.
cta.note: Free · No commitment · Written shortlist in 5 business days
